choices.text: Ask where they were
Ask what they saw
Probe the timeline
Challenge their alibi
Ask about their connection to the victim
Ask about the suspect's recent behavior
Press on the contradictions
Suggest you understand why it happened

nodes.text: I was with {suspect} earlier that evening. We were together for part of it.

choices.text: For how long?
And after that?

nodes.text: A few hours. Until around {time_phrase}, I think. We parted ways. It was a normal evening. Nothing remarkable.
After that I went home. {suspect} went their own way. I do not know exactly where. I was not keeping tabs on them.
I did not see anything unusual. It was quiet where we were. Nothing to report.

choices.text: Think carefully. Anything at all?
You are certain nothing stood out?

nodes.text: I told you. Nothing. We had a conversation, we had drinks, people came and went. A completely ordinary evening.
Yes. Nothing stood out. If something had, I would tell you. I want this resolved as much as anyone.
Around {time_phrase}, more or less. I was not watching the clock. It was not that kind of evening.

choices.text: More or less is not good enough.
What were you doing at that time?

nodes.text: I cannot give you a minute-by-minute account of a casual evening. I have told you roughly when. That is what I have.
We were talking. Sitting. {suspect} was with me. I have said this. We were not doing anything that required timestamps.
{suspect} was with me. I have said so. That should mean something.

choices.text: Can anyone else confirm you were both there?
Your account of the timing does not hold.

nodes.text: There were people around. I cannot give you names right now. But we were not invisible. Someone will have noticed.
We were not attached at the hip all evening. There were moments. Brief ones. I was not tracking every minute.
I knew them. Not as well as some. There was history there, not all of it pleasant.

choices.text: What kind of history?
Was there conflict recently?

nodes.text: Complicated history. Things that go back a while. I am not going to air all of that for you. It is not relevant.
There were tensions. That is all I will say. The victim was not always easy to deal with. That is the honest truth.
{suspect} has been under a great deal of stress lately. That does not make them guilty of anything.

choices.text: What kind of stress?
Did the stress seem connected to the victim?

nodes.text: Personal things. Financial things. The kind of pressure that builds up over time and does not have one single cause.
There were complications between them. I am not going to say more than that. It would not be fair to characterize it.
I have told you what I know. If my account does not match someone else's, then maybe that someone else has their own reasons.

choices.text: You are changing details each time.
I am giving you a chance to clarify.

nodes.text: I am not changing anything. You are looking for gaps that are not there. I have been consistent.
Maybe I have the time slightly wrong. I have admitted that. But the substance — what I saw, where I was — that is the truth.
What are you trying to get me to say?

choices.text: That the victim may have pushed someone too far.
That sometimes people are backed into corners.
That someone may have been protecting another person.
That this may not have gone the way anyone intended.

nodes.text: The victim was not an easy person. I will say that much. There are things I could tell you. But I am not sure this is the right moment.
Yes. Situations can get away from people. Pressure builds. I am not excusing anything. I am just saying it is not always simple.
If someone did this to protect another person... that is not the same as malice. You understand the difference. I hope you do.
Things can go too far without anyone planning it. That happens. People panic. Decisions get made in seconds.
